ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
estop: verb. To impede or bar by estoppel.
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
noose: noun. An adjustable loop of rope, such as the one placed around the neck in hangings, or the one at the end of a lasso.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
not: adverb. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
onset: noun. The initial phase of a disease or condition, in which symptoms first become apparent.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
oops: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.
ope: interjection. An exclamation of surprise; oops.
opes: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ope
oppose: verb. To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.
opts: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of opt
pen: noun. An enclosure (enclosed area) used to contain domesticated animals, especially sheep or cattle.
peon: noun. A lowly person; a peasant or serf; a labourer who is obliged to do menial work.
pep: verb. To inject with energy and enthusiasm.
pepo: noun. A fruit of plants of the gourd family Cucurbitaceae, possessing a hard rind and producing many seeds in a single, central, pulpy
pes: noun. the foot of a human
peso: noun. A former unit of currency in Spain and Spain's colonies, worth 8 reales; the Spanish dollar.
pest: noun. A pestilence, i.e. a deadly epidemic, a deadly plague.
pesto: noun. A sauce, especially for pasta, originating from the Genoa region in Italy, made from basil, garlic, pine nuts, olive oil and cheese
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
poet: noun. A person who writes poems.
pone: noun. A writ in law used by the superior courts to remove cases from inferior courts.
pons: noun. A bridge-like tissue connecting two parts of an organ.
poon: noun. Any of several East Indian trees of the genus Calophyllum, yielding a light, hard wood used for masts, spars, etc.
poop: verb. To make a short blast on a horn.
pop: noun. A loud, sharp sound, as of a cork coming out of a bottle, especially when the contents are pressurized by fizziness.
pope: noun. An honorary title of the Roman Catholic bishop of Rome as father and head of his church, a sovereign of the Vatican city state.
pose: noun. Common cold, head cold; catarrh.
post: noun. A long dowel or plank protruding from the ground; a fencepost; a lightpost.
postpone: verb. To delay or put off an event, appointment, etc.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
sept: noun. A clan, tribe, or family, proceeding from a common progenitor; especially, one of the ancient clans of Ireland.
set: verb. To put (something) down, to rest.
seton: noun. A few silk threads or horsehairs, or a strip of linen etc., introduced beneath the skin by a knife or needle, so as to induce
snoop: verb. To be devious and cunning so as not to be seen.
snoot: noun. An elitist or snobbish person.
snot: noun. Mucus, especially mucus from the nose.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
soon: adjective. Short in length of time from the present.
soot: noun. Fine black or dull brown particles of amorphous carbon and tar, produced by the incomplete combustion of coal, oil etc.
spent: adjective. Consumed, used up, exhausted, depleted.
spoon: noun. An implement for eating or serving; a scooped utensil whose long handle is straight, in contrast to a ladle.
spot: noun. A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.
steno: noun. a stenographer, someone whose job is to take dictation in shorthand
step: noun. An advance or movement made from one foot to the other; a pace.
stone: noun. A hard earthen substance that can form rocks; especially, such substance when regarded as a building material.
stoop: noun. A stooping, bent position of the body.
stop: noun. ITU & IMO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for full stop / period.
stope: noun. A mining excavation in the form of a terrace of steps.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
toe: noun. Each of the five digits on the end of the human foot.
ton: noun. Any of various units of mass, originally notionally equal to the contents of a tun, particularly: The short ton of 2000 pounds (about
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
top: noun. The highest or uppermost part of something.
tope: verb. To drink excessively; to get drunk.
topos: noun. A literary theme or motif; a rhetorical convention or formula.
tops: adverb. At the very most; as a maximum.
epopt: noun. An initiate in the Eleusinian Mysteries; one who has attended the epopteia.
oop: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.
oose: noun. Fluff, particularly from a textile source such as cotton or wool.
oppo: noun. A friend, associate or colleague.
osone: noun. any 1,2- ketoaldose, especially one derived by hydrolysis of an osazone
oto: noun. An otocinclus (freshwater fish, genus Otocinclus).
pon: noun. A call for forming a set of three identical tiles using a discarded tile.
pont: verb. to pose until nearly frozen in all sorts of uncomfortable positions.
ponto: noun. A traditional short Portuguese unit of length usually about equal to 0.2 mm.
poo: noun. Alternative spelling of pooh: an instance of saying " poo ".
poot: noun. A fart, perhaps a relatively quiet one.
posnet: noun. A little basin; a porringer; a skillet.
sope: noun. A traditional Mexican food consisting of a masa base with various savory toppings.
sten: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Sten
stoep: noun. A raised veranda in front of a house.
topepo: noun. A reputed hybrid between the tomato and the sweet pepper.
tos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Highland Totonac.
tose: verb. To pull apart or asunder; touse.
